zh.lifeSummary: 當Maria Dorothea Shoemaker於大約1758年出生在美國馬里蘭州弗雷德里克時，她的父親Bartholomeus Schumacher30歲，母親Anna Maria Barbara38歲。她於從1781年到1783年與Johann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner"在美國馬里蘭州弗雷德里克結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1831年2月7日在美國俄亥俄州可倫比納亞特 堂史普享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maria Dorothea Shoemaker于大约1758年出生在美国马里兰州弗雷德里克时，她的父亲Bartholomeus Schumacher30岁，母亲Anna Maria Barbara38岁。她于从1781年到1783年与Johann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner"在美国马里兰州弗雷德里克结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1831年2月7日在美国俄亥俄州可倫比納亞特 堂史普享年74岁。

en.Center Twp. Columbiana Co. OH: Center Township, Columbiana, Ohio, United States

nameMeanings.name: Shoemaker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form (translation into English) of Dutch Schoemaker , German and Jewish Schumacher , Slovenian Šuštar (see Sustar ) or Greek Papoutsis .

variants.name: Papoutsis
meanings: Greek: occupational name for a shoemaker, papoutsis, a derivative of papoutsi ‘shoe’, a word of Turkish and ultimately Persian origin. Compare Shoemaker .

variants.name: Schoemaker
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a shoemaker, Middle Dutch scoemaker(e). This surname is also found in Germany.

nameMeanings.name: Maria
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: Dorothea
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of a post-classical Greek name, from dōron ‘gift’ + theos ‘god’ (the same elements as in Theodora , but in reverse order). The masculine form Dorotheus was borne by several early Christian saints, the feminine by only two minor ones, but only the girl's name has survived. In modern use in the English-speaking world it represents either a 19th-century Latinization of Dorothy or a learned reborrowing.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
In 1781, Maryland donated land to be used for part of Washington, D. C.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Making States Equal
historicEvents.description: The Missouri Compromise helped provide the entrance of Maine as a free state and Missouri as a slave state into the United States. As part of the compromise, slavery was prohibited north of the 36°30′ parallel, excluding Missouri.

en.lifeSummary: When Maria Dorothea Shoemaker was born about 1758, in Frederick, Maryland, United States, her father, Bartholomeus Schumacher, was 30 and her mother, Anna Maria Barbara, was 38. She married Johann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner" from 1781 to 1783, in Frederick, Maryland, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 7 February 1831, in Center Township, Columbiana, Ohio, United States, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maria Dorothea Shoemaker est née environ 1758, à Frederick, Maryland, États-Unis, son père, Bartholomeus Schumacher, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Anna Maria Barbara, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé Johann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner" du 1781 au 1783, à Frederick, Maryland, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 février 1831, à Center Township, Columbiana, Ohio, États-Unis, à l’âge de 74 ans.

Sie hat von 1781 bis 1783 in Frederick, Maryland, Vereinigte Staaten Johann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner" geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 7. Februar 1831 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Center Township, Columbiana, Ohio, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Maria Dorothea Shoemakerが約1758年にアメリカ合衆国メリーランド州フレデリックで生まれた時，父Bartholomeus Schumacherは30歳，母Anna Maria Barbaraは38歳であった。から1781年まで1783年にアメリカ合衆国メリーランド州フレデリックでJohann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner"と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1831年2月7日にアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州コルンビアナセンター トウンシュイップで74歳で死去した。

ko.Center Twp. Columbiana Co. OH: 미국 오하이오 주 콜럼비아나 세터 타운시프
ko.lifeSummary: Maria Dorothea Shoemaker(이)가 대략 1758년 미국 메릴랜드 주 프레더릭크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Bartholomeus Schumacher(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anna Maria Barbara(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1781년 부터 1783년 까지 미국 메릴랜드 주 프레더릭크에서 Johann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner"(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1831년 2월 7일 미국 오하이오 주 콜럼비아나 세터 타운시프에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maria Dorothea Shoemaker nasceu aproximadamente 1758, em Frederick, Marilândia, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Bartholomeus Schumacher, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Anna Maria Barbara, tinha 38 anos. Ela casou-se com Johann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner" de 1781 a 1783, em Frederick, Marilândia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de fevereiro de 1831, em Center Township, Columbiana, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América, com 74 anos.

ru.Center Twp. Columbiana Co. OH: кентир тоуншип, колумбяна, Огайо, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maria Dorothea Shoemaker родилась около 1758 г. в местности фредирикк, Мэриленд, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Bartholomeus Schumacher было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Maria Barbara, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Johann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner" с 1781 по 1783 в местности фредирикк, Мэриленд, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 7 февраля 1831 г. в местности кентир тоуншип, колумбяна, Огайо, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 74 лет.

es.Center Twp. Columbiana Co. OH: Center Township, Columbiana, Ohio, Estados Unidos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maria Dorothea Shoemaker nació aproximadamente 1758, en Frederick, Maryland, Estados Unidos, su papá, Bartholomeus Schumacher, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Anna Maria Barbara, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Johann Jacob Hopffner "Hephner" desde 1781 hasta 1783, en Frederick, Maryland, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 7 de febrero de 1831, en Center Township, Columbiana, Ohio, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 74 años.
